timeline.label: FY2025 net income $1.34B; continued share repurchases; cash $1.916B vs senior notes $909M.
Deposit impairment charges rose to $75.9M (from $7.2M in 2024) as housing affordability stress increased cancellation risk.
Controlled ~207,000 lots via LPAs with $851M net contract-land deposits; capital-light land strategy vs land-heavy peers.
Aggressive share count reduction continued; per-share compounding remains central capital-allocation lever.
Post-GFC housing recovery reinforced NVR's deposit-based lot control model in Mid-Atlantic and Southeast markets.
Financial crisis validated forfeitable-deposit land strategy: abandoned lots with limited balance-sheet land risk vs peers.
NVR adopted lot-purchase agreement model, controlling finished lots through options rather than owning land.
Ryan Homes / NVHomes regional builder roots in affordable move-up and first-time buyer segments.

industry.structure: U.S. homebuilding is fragmented but consolidating; land and labor are the binding constraints; NVR's deposit-based lot control reduces capital intensity versus land-heavy builders (DHI, LEN, PHM).
industry.share_shift: Large builders gained share post-2008 via scale, mortgage captive, and land pipelines; NVR leads on returns and buybacks in its regional footprint.
industry.trend: 2025 affordability pressure lifted cancellation rates (17%) and deposit impairments; mortgage rates and regional mix drive near-term margins; lot option model limits but does not eliminate cyclical risk.
